title: microlepia kerrii
intro: Microlepia kerrii is a species of fern that is native to Southeast Asia, particularly in Malaysia and Thailand, and is also known as the "Malaysian fern". This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Microlepia kerrii include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory problems. In traditional herbal preparations, the plant is typically used to make teas or infusions, with the leaves being dried and then steeped in hot water to release its medicinal properties.
